zh.Leighton, Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Englan: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾麗頓
zh.Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, England: 英國藍卡爾貝福 霍爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Edith Pincerna Le Boteler於1163年出生在英國藍卡爾沃林頓時，她的父親Sir Richard le Boteler33歲，母親Beatrix de Villiers28歲。她於大約1181年與Richard de Molyneux在英國藍卡爾澤福頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1187年在英國藍卡爾澤福頓享年24歲。

zh-hans.Leighton, Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Englan: 英国英格兰史洛普爾麗頓
zh-hans.Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, England: 英国藍卡爾貝福 霍爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edith Pincerna Le Boteler于1163年出生在英国藍卡爾沃林頓时，她的父亲Sir Richard le Boteler33岁，母亲Beatrix de Villiers28岁。她于大约1181年与Richard de Molyneux在英国藍卡爾澤福頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1187年在英国藍卡爾澤福頓享年24岁。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Edith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English female personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + gӯð ‘strife’. This was borne by a daughter ( 961–984 ) of Edgar the Peaceful. (She was named in accordance with the common Old English practice of repeating name elements within a family.) She spent her short life in a convent, and is regarded as a saint.

variants.name: Edythe
meanings: From an Old English female personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + gӯð ‘strife’. This was borne by a daughter ( 961–984 ) of Edgar the Peaceful. (She was named in accordance with the common Old English practice of repeating name elements within a family.) She spent her short life in a convent, and is regarded as a saint.

en.lifeSummary: When Edith Pincerna Le Boteler was born in 1163, in Warrington, Lancashire, England, her father, Sir Richard le Boteler, was 33 and her mother, Beatrix de Villiers, was 28. She married Richard de Molyneux about 1181, in Sefton, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1187, in Sefton, Lancashire, England, at the age of 24.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edith Pincerna Le Boteler est née en 1163, à Warrington, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard le Boteler, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Beatrix de Villiers, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Richard de Molyneux environ 1181, à Sefton, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1187, à Sefton, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 24 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edith Pincerna Le Boteler 1163 in Warrington, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Richard le Boteler 33 und ihre Mutter Beatrix de Villiers 28. Sie hat um 1181 in Sefton, Lancashire, England Richard de Molyneux geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb 1187 im Alter von 24 Jahren in Sefton, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edith Pincerna Le Boteler è nata il nel 1163 nel Warrington, Lancashire, England, sua padre, Sir Richard le Boteler, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Beatrix de Villiers, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Richard de Molyneux circa 1181, nel Sefton, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1187, nel Sefton, Lancashire, England, all'età di 24 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Edith Pincerna Le Botelerが1163年にイギリスランカシュアイアワーリントンで生まれた時，父Sir Richard le Botelerは33歳，母Beatrix de Villiersは28歳であった。約1181年にイギリスランカシュアイアセフトンでRichard de Molyneuxと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1187年にイギリスランカシュアイアセフトンで24歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Edith Pincerna Le Boteler(이)가 1163년 영국 랭카실르 워링턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Richard le Boteler(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrix de Villiers(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 대략 1181년 영국 랭카실르 세프턴에서 Richard de Molyneux(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1187년 영국 랭카실르 세프턴에서 24세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edith Pincerna Le Boteler nasceu em 1163, em Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Richard le Boteler, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Beatrix de Villiers, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard de Molyneux aproximadamente 1181, em Sefton, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1187, em Sefton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 24 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edith Pincerna Le Boteler родилась в 1163 г. в местности варрингтон, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Richard le Boteler было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrix de Villiers, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard de Molyneux около 1181 г. в местности сефтон, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1187 г. в местности сефтон, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 24 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edith Pincerna Le Boteler nació en 1163, en Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard le Boteler, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Beatrix de Villiers, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Richard de Molyneux aproximadamente 1181, en Sefton, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1187, en Sefton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 24 años.
